senses.definition: to do something that makes a person appear foolish in front of others

examples.x: He made a monkey of me by convincing me to wear a ridiculous costume to the party.
Don't let him make a monkey out of you with his tricks and pranks.
He thought he could outsmart us, but we ended up making a monkey of him in the negotiation.

senses.definition: to make fun of a person or thing in order to make them look silly

examples.x: During the party, we all poked fun at John for his funny dance moves.
The comedian poked fun at current events in his stand-up routine.
The children like to poke fun at their teacher's quirky habits.

word_list.pronunciation: tňąe…™k √į…ô mňą…™k mňą…™ki ňĆa ä…ĺ…ôv ňĆ…õsbňąiňź

senses.definition: to tease a person by copying what they do, trick them into believing something, etc., often in a way that is not friendly

examples.x: Don't take the mickey out of him; he's just trying to learn.

senses.definition: a person or thing so silly or ridiculous that everyone makes fun of

examples.x: After his failed attempt at stand-up comedy, he became the laughing stock of the comedy club.
The new product's design was so bizarre that it became the laughing stock of the industry.
She didn't want to be the laughing stock of the school, so she practiced her speech tirelessly.
The politician's embarrassing gaffe made him the laughing stock of the entire nation.
Becoming the laughing stock of the office was the last thing he wanted, so he double-checked his presentation

senses.definition: (particularly of a joke) in a way that causes harm to someone or makes them embarrassed

examples.x: He told a joke at her expense, which left her feeling humiliated in front of the entire group.
Making fun of his appearance during the presentation was completely uncalled for and hurtful‚ÄĒit was humor at his expense.
They played a prank on him at his expense, but he took it in good spirits and laughed along with them.
She felt like everyone was laughing at her expense when her voice cracked during the important speech.
Mocking someone's misfortune or mistakes is never funny, especially when it's done at their expense.

senses.definition: to offer a humorous or witty comment, often with the aim of ridiculing disrespecting someone or something

examples.x: She always knows how to make a crack that lightens the mood during tense meetings.
John can't resist making a crack about the weather whenever it starts raining during our picnics.
Even in serious situations, he manages to make a crack to ease the tension.
When he's in a good mood, he'll make a crack about anything and everything, keeping us entertained all day.

senses.definition: to persistently and relentlessly bother, tease, or annoy someone, often in a playful or mocking manner

examples.x: I wish my coworker would stop criticizing everything I do; he's really breaking my stones.
Why does he always have to break my balls about being five minutes late?
She keeps asking me the same question over and over, and it's starting to break my stones.
The constant nagging and complaining from my neighbor are really breaking my balls.
I can't stand it when he's always trying to break my stones with his sarcastic remarks.
